{"step_by_step_thinking": "According to the AHA 2010 Guidelines, adrenaline (epinephrine) is used in CPCR. Vasopressin is also mentioned as an alternative to epinephrine in certain circumstances. Atropine is not typically used in CPCR as it is primarily used to treat bradycardia. Amiodarone is used in CPCR for the treatment of ventricular fibrillation or pulseless ventricular tachycardia. Therefore, the drug that is not used in CPCR is Atropine.", "answer_choice": "C"}
